analysis_title: Comparative GEV Analysis on R_comp Metric for Multiplicative Functions
research_hypothesis: The heavy-tailed signature of R_comp extremes (GEV shape parameter ξ > 0) is unique to non-multiplicative functions. All multiplicative functions should exhibit ξ ≤ 0.

chi4.name: L(χ₄)
chi4.coefficients: χ₄ mod 5: χ₄(n)=0 if gcd(n,5)≠1, +1 if n≡±1(mod 5), -1 if n≡±2(mod 5)
chi4.note: Bootstrap instability observed (51.3% success rate). MM method used due to better fit quality (AIC=20.1 vs 135.9 for MLE).

f_rand.name: f_rand
f_rand.coefficients: Random multiplicative function with seed=42: random ±1 assignment to primes
f_rand.note: CONTRADICTS hypothesis: exhibits heavy-tailed behavior (ξ > 0) despite being multiplicative.

key_findings: Riemann ζ: ξ = -0.140 (95% CI: [-0.253, -0.014]) - bounded tail, consistent with hypothesis
L(χ₄): ξ = 0.009 (95% CI: [-0.667, 0.200]) - Gumbel-type, consistent with hypothesis but high uncertainty
f_rand: ξ = 0.213 (95% CI: [0.012, 0.379]) - heavy-tailed, INCONSISTENT with hypothesis
Heavy-tailed signature NOT unique to non-multiplicative functions
Random multiplicative functions can exhibit heavy tails similar to L_DH

computational_details.numerical_precision: Kahan compensated summation for relative error < 10^-15
computational_details.mobius_and_omega: Computed via sieve for all n ≤ N
